mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative alpha in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 45 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity facing higher financing costs, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of observations during tightening periods with falling industrial production, or the mechanism shows no predictive power in this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of months, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 305.0, inflation persistence forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy longer, increasing equity discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of threshold-crossing months, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth accelerates while real personal consumption expenditures decelerates indicates weak household demand despite aggregate output gains, leading to inventory buildup and negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which boosts near-term corporate cash flows and generates positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of recovery-phase observations with rising industrial production, or the signal exhibits a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.
